REFERENCE: This reference exhibits multiple indicators of fabrication/attribution error. ALL authors (Lehnert BP, Ginty DD, Panzeri S, Harvey CD, Emanuel AJ) are well-known NEUROSCIENTISTS who study somatosensory and cold-sensing neurons — they have no expertise in infectious disease, epidemiology, or cold chain logistics. The title 'Cold chain logistics: a possible mode of SARS-CoV-2 transmission?' appears to be a fabrication that conflates the real paper's topic (cold-sensing neurons) with SARS-CoV-2 transmission. The DOI (10.1038/s41586-021-04094-x), PMID (34689880), and PMCID (PMC9835956) likely correspond to the actual neuroscience paper by these authors published in Nature (2021), which is about **somatosensory neurons and cold sensation**, not COVID-19 logistics. The issue number (7890) is in an unusual parenthetical format without 'No.' or 'Issue' prefix. Overall, the citation machinery (DOI, PMID, author list, journal, date) appears real, but the **title is maliciously altered** to create a fake reference about SARS-CoV-2.
